category_title: Students with cerebral palsy and hearing impairment focused on communication goals
student_description: Jason is an anxious student with low initial motivation who frequently worries about holding his classmates back during group work. He uniquely requires capability-matched roles, direct face-to-face validation to accept his weaknesses, and structured opportunities to build incrementally on peers' ideas rather than generating them from scratch.

criteria.rationale: Providing a predictable structure with ample time reduces his anxiety about rushing or holding others back.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan includes structured lesson phases with dedicated, unhurried time for practice and plenary evaluation.

criteria.rationale: Regular, structured self-evaluation interviews help Jason gradually build self-monitoring skills and overcome chronic low motivation.
criteria.check: The tutor incorporates recurring, interactive goal-setting and self-evaluation sessions (e.g., termly interviews) into the student's learning trajectory.

criteria.rationale: Jason needs intensive, gradual exposure to new skills across different subjects to build enduring confidence.
criteria.check: The tutor coordinates with other subjects or contexts to ensure the student practices the same target skills consistently over time.
